evals.name: command-surface-restores-existing-commands
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:spec, /tk:gap, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff, and /tk:meta-feedback as active user-facing commands. Should explain that handoff and meta-feedback were preserved existing commands, not newly invented commands. Should explain that spec/gap write branch-local working memory, reflect creates durable insight, handoff creates continuation context, and meta-feedback extracts generalized TigerKit improvement feedback from session history.
expectations: Treats handoff as restored existing continuation command
Treats meta-feedback as restored existing session-history improvement command

evals.name: gap-single-execution
evals.prompt: Evaluate the /tk:gap command surface. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should describe /tk:gap as a single /tk:gap execution. Should not describe lite and strict as active user-facing quality modes. Should say --lite and --strict are compatibility flags only if mentioned. Should describe analysisDepth as direct, bounded, expanded, or exhaustive-capped. Should preserve existing /tk:gap command and not create a new skill or remove an existing skill.

evals.name: gap-analysis-depth-heuristic
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap analysis depth selection. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should choose direct only for one explicit local surface with no API/DTO/state/auth/payment/data mutation/shared component/ambiguity trigger. Should choose bounded for single screen/component/command with nearby 1-depth usage lookup or representative samples up to 3. Should choose expanded for shared component, design-system, API/DTO/state transition, source conflict risk, inaccessible reference, ambiguous Product/API/Design decision, or divergent similar implementations. Should choose exhaustive-capped for P0/P1 candidate, auth/permission/payment/data mutation/destructive action, release gate, or cross-module impact. Hard triggers should run before riskScore tie-breakers. Should record analysisDepth, depthReasons, riskScore, sideEffectConfidence, verificationEscalation, compatibilityFlags, dispatchPlan, dispatchSkips, and heuristicProof.analysisDepth. Explicit --analysis-depth should not lower the heuristic-required minimum depth for risky surfaces; lower requested depth should be escalated and recorded. Should not call the choice lite or strict mode.
expectations: Hard triggers run before riskScore tie-breakers

evals.name: gap-performance-proof
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap performance proof requirements for the single /tk:gap execution. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require a measured performance proof rather than a vague estimate. Should use the contract-derived critical path proxy when runtime wall-clock instrumentation is unavailable. Should require performance.improvementRatio >= 1.3, with performance.baselineCriticalPathScore, performance.currentCriticalPathScore, performance.agentCriticalPathGroups, performance.deterministicStageGroups, performance.runProcedureSteps, performance.measurementMethod, and heuristicProof.speed recorded in the run receipt or manifest. Current optimized contract target should show performance.baselineCriticalPathScore = 87.1, performance.currentCriticalPathScore <= 49.2, and minimumTargetImprovementRatio >= 1.77 by contract-derived-critical-path-proxy. Should calculate baseline and current with the same proxy weights. Credited dispatchSkips may count only when credited, criticalPathDelta, and evidenceCoveragePreserved are recorded. Should not claim success from phrases such as expected, estimated, or likely.
expectations: Requires measurementMethod and critical path components
Requires credited dispatch skip fields before counting skip benefit

evals.name: gap-consumer-fallback-not-producer-absence
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate that claims an API does not provide a required value, but cites only frontend fallback/default/mapper evidence. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not accept the candidate as P0/P1/P2 and should not create SourceConflict from consumer-only evidence. If API contract, schema, endpoint response, serializer, data model, backend fixture, or owner-confirmed producer behavior is provided or discoverable in the repo, it should inspect that producer surface before asking the user. Should reject or downgrade with missing_producer_evidence, missing_evidence, or unverifiable only after available producer surfaces are checked. Should state producer-side evidence is required, tell the user what producer evidence was checked and what is still missing, and create a ClarificationNeeded confirmation request when the missing producer evidence blocks the gap decision.
expectations: Checks provided or discoverable producer evidence before asking user
Uses missing_producer_evidence only after available producer surfaces are checked
Does not accept SourceConflict without producer evidence
Creates ClarificationNeeded for missing producer evidence when decision-blocking

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-blocks-judge-accept-path
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate where Product/API/Design source priority is ambiguous. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not silently choose the likely interpretation. Should route the item to SourceConflict or ClarificationNeeded before accepted finding path. Should continue other clear checks. Should record evidence, impact, recommendation, and status for the clarification.

evals.name: gap-final-receipt-single-execution
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap stdout receipt for a completed run. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should output analysis depth, expansion reasons, verification escalation, dispatch skip summary, Candidate Intake Gate summary, report path, P0/P1/P2 counts, source conflict count, clarification-needed count, rejected/downgraded count, numeric performance proof, and compact Actionable Findings and Rejected/Downgraded tables using short run-local Ref values instead of long canonical Candidate/Finding IDs. Should not output executed preset, recommended preset, or Strict executed as primary labels.
expectations: Includes dispatch skip and Candidate Intake Gate summaries
Compact tables use short Ref values instead of long canonical IDs

evals.name: meta-feedback-uses-session-history-for-gap-improvements
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:meta-feedback after a session where the user said gap was slow, backend findings had false positives, mode wording was confusing, and ambiguous UI/API decisions should be resolved with the user. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use the session history as evidence, generalize the feedback without exposing project assets, and propose TigerKit command or skill improvements. It should capture performance friction for gap analysis depth, dispatch gate needs, candidate evidence precision gates for stale file:line/module-path coordinates, producer-absence evidence gates for backend false-positive reduction, ambiguity consent gate guidance, and UI clarification via tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups. It should not create repo rule patches like reflect and should not output raw user quotes, paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels.
expectations: Uses session history as the evidence source
Generalizes candidate evidence precision gate improvement
Mentions tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups for UI clarification
Proposes TigerKit command or skill improvement
Does not expose raw user quotes
Does not expose paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels
Does not behave like /tk:reflect repo rule patching

evals.name: handoff-remains-continuation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:handoff after a branch has spec and gap artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should preserve /tk:handoff as the existing continuation command. It should write .claude/handoffs/current.md by default, include Reader Guide and Resume Prompt, optionally mention latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths when observed, and create a dated archive only when archive=true or an explicit archive request is present.
expectations: Can reference latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths
Does not create dated archive without archive=true or explicit archive request

evals.name: reflect-applies-directly-to-rules-and-claude
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:reflect after branch-local gap runs produced durable repo-wide insight candidates. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default to apply=true, read branch-local TigerKit memory, write directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/* depending on target scope, treat --dry-run and --apply=false as preview-only aliases, avoid modifying source code, dedupe repeated insights, and avoid creating a separate tigerkit-reflections sidecar file.
expectations: Does not create tigerkit-reflections sidecar file

evals.name: gap-combined-heuristic-proof-claim
evals.prompt: Evaluate whether /tk:gap may claim the current 1.3x improvement target for false-positive reduction, speed, and analysis depth. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require heuristicProof.requiredImprovementRatio = 1.3. Should require heuristicProof.falsePositive.metric, denominator, baselinePredicateScore, currentPredicateScore, improvementRatio, and claimAllowed. Should require heuristicProof.speed.metric, denominator, baselineCriticalPathScore, currentCriticalPathScore, improvementRatio, and claimAllowed. Should require heuristicProof.analysisDepth.metric, denominator, baselineTriggerCoverage, currentTriggerCoverage, improvementRatio, and claimAllowed. Should allow the combined claim only when all three improvementRatio values are >= 1.3 and all three claimAllowed values are true. Should reject the combined claim if consumer-only producer evidence, uncredited dispatch skips, or risk-score-before-hard-trigger depth selection is used.
expectations: Rejects combined claim if any subproof is below threshold
